message:
The other day, I realized it had been way too long since I tested our smoke alarms. So, I gathered the kids in the kitchen for a little drill. I set a timer on my phone and told them we were having a "fire safety party!" 

They giggled as I pushed the test button, and when that loud beep echoed through the house, they jumped a little but then burst into laughter. We talked about our escape plan, which included the all-important "meet at the mailbox" spot. It turned into a cozy event with hot cocoa afterward, and honestly, I think we all felt a bit more connected—and safer too! 

Sometimes, turning preparedness into a fun family moment makes it stick better in our minds. Have you ever turned a safety drill into a fun family activity?
